---
title: "DictLoader"
description: "Load geometries from a Python dictionary and align with GRB reference data"
author: "Karel Dieussaert"
date: "2026-01-07"
categories: [Loader]
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
jupyter: python3
---
# DictLoader
This example shows [DictLoader](../reference/DictLoader.qmd), useful when geometries already exist as Python objects (`{id: geometry}`).

## What you learn

1. Build an `Aligner` with explicit CRS.
2. Load thematic geometry from a dictionary.
3. Load GRB reference data.
4. Run processing for one distance and inspect WKT outputs.

## Typical use case

Use this when your application already has geometries in memory (for example after ETL or preprocessing) and you want minimal loader overhead.

## Notes

- This example uses one large geometry and prints `result`, `result_diff_plus`, and `result_diff_min`.
- If you need attributes in output, combine this with examples that include metadata enrichment.

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "basic example"
{{< include ../../examples/example_dictloader.py >}}
```
